channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: The World Cup quarter-finals are here! Your guide to every tie
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: From 48, we're now down to just eight. The World Cup quarter-finals begin today - and we're one step closer to knowing who will lift the trophy on July 19.

item.title: Soldiers now earn N100,000; Justice Crack staged food video, says Defence minister
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "When they started, a soldier was collecting N49,000 monthly. We tried so hard; now he's collecting N100,000," he said. The post Soldiers now earn N100,000; Justice Crack staged food video, says Defence minister appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Kwara 2027: APC Group Rejects Olasumbo’s Rep Candidacy
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A wave of discontent has continued to pervade the All Progressives Congress (APC) in the Ekiti/ Irepodun/Isin/Oke-Ero Federal Constituency of Kwara State, as a prominent group, under the aegis of Concerned APC Stakeholders, has vehemently opposed the purported nomination of Hon. Florence Olasumbo Oy

item.title: FCT: 70 percent of projects I completed were awarded 16 years ago – Wike
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, FCT, Nyesom Wike, has disclosed that projects completed by the FCT Administration under his leadership were awarded 15 to 16 years ago. Wike explained that the FCTA under his leadership took up these project in line with President Bola Tinubu’s ma

item.title: OpenAI To Release GPT-5.6 AI Models Publicly Today After US Approval
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI said its latest powerful artificial intelligence model series will be released to the public today (Thursday), after the US government reportedly approved a broader launch. The company’s new GPT-5.6 offerings and other cutting-edge AI models, including Anthropic’s Mythos series,

item.title: NPFL: Insurance boss Boboye lays down marker ahead new season
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Bendel Insurance technical adviser, Kennedy Boboye has tasked his players to improve their performance in the 2026/27 season, DAILY POST reports. The Benin City club finished in fifth position last season with 55 points, missing out on a place on the continent. The players resumed pre-season trainin

item.title: Nigerian soldiers’ salary raised from N49,000 to N100,000— Defence Minister
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Minister of Defence, Christopher Musa, has said the Federal Government has increased the monthly salary of Nigerian soldiers from N49,000 to N100,000 as The post Nigerian soldiers’ salary raised from N49,000 to N100,000— Defence Minister appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Slovakia introduces 120-day national visas, tougher work permit rules from July 15
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Slovakia is set to overhaul its immigration system from July 15, 2026, with a package of reforms that extends national visa validity, gives foreign workers more time to secure new employment after losing a job, centralizes visa processing, and tightens documentation requirements for work permit appl

item.title: Tinubu Approves Reconstruction of Ado-Ajan-Ilumoba-Ikole Road
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has approved the reconstruction of the Ado-Ijan- Ilumoba- Ikole road in Ekiti State. The road project is to be funded through the Renewed Hope Infrastructure Project

item.title: SSS alleges journalist Zainab Sodiq detained for possessing drone without ‘documents’
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: THE State Security Services (SSS) has confirmed the detention of journalist Zainab Sodiq, saying she is being investigated over the alleged possession of a drone without the required authorisation, a claim disputed by activist Omoyele Sowore. The SSS, in a statement on Wednesday, July 8, by its Depu

item.title: Karim Adeyemi to Barcelona: Nigerian descent 90% close to La Liga move – what is left?
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Karim Adeyemi is edging closer to a move to Spanish champions Barcelona, with the Borussia Dortmund forward now very close to completing the transfer, Soccernet.ng reports. The attacker of Nigerian-descent joined Dortmund from Red Bull Salzburg in 2022 and has established himself as one of the club'

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Kano State chapter of the All Progressives Congress (APC) has expressed confidence that the infrastructure projects executed by the The post Tinubu’s projects will secure 2027 victory — Kano APC appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: NiMet Forecasts Rain, Flood Risk, Thunderstorms For Today
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: According to the forecast, the wet weather is expected to affect states across the northern, central, and southern regions, with thunderstorms, moderate rainfall, strong winds, and reduced visibility expected in several locations. The post NiMet Forecasts Rain, Flood Risk, Thunderstorms For Today ap

item.title: Where innovation meets healthcare: Hadejia’s new digital ecosystem, By Mukhtar Ya’u Madobi
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: In Hadejia, Nigeria has taken more than a step towards building new institutions. It has offered a glimpse into a future where technology, research, entrepreneurship, and compassionate healthcare work together to improve lives, stimulate economic growth, and accelerate sustainable national developme

item.title: 'Sack him' - Stokes reacts as ICC look into possible retirement video rule breach
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Ben Stokes appears to have made light of the International Cricket Council (ICC) contacting the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) over a potential rule breach linked to his retirement announcement.

item.title: Troops Rescue Two, Arrest Five After ISWAP Attack On Borno Base
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have confirmed the death of an officer, following an ISWAP attack in Borno, the rescue of two abductees; arrest of five suspects and recovery of over N2.74m, one rifle, ammunition and other items across Adamawa and Yobe states. The soldier was killed after suspected ISW

item.description: According to the latest available market data, the naira continues to trade within a relatively stable range in recent sessions. The post Naira To Dollar Exchange Rate Today, July 9, 2026 appeared first on Channels Television.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Grammy Award-winning Nigerian Afrobeats singer, Damini Ogulu, popularly known as Burna Boy has been scheduled to perform alongside American superstars, Justin Bieber, Shakira and Madonna at the inaugural 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show. Announcing the development on Wednesday, FIFA said the

item.title: Russia’s War Machine Now Runs On Africa’s Betrayed Youth
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Moscow’s triumphant rhetoric about victory in Ukraine increasingly clashes with the grim reality on the battlefield. Behind the speeches lies a Russian army plagued by internal collapse, brutal punishments, and dwindling morale. As Russian soldiers refuse to fight, the Kremlin has turned its gaze ou

item.title: UITH doctors down tools over assault on member
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Medical doctors at the University of Ilorin Teaching Hospital, Kwara State, have suspended their services over alleged physical assault by a woman who lost her child on one of their members in the health facility. Following the loss, the mother lost control and attacked the doctor over the incident.

item.title: Abuja’s Transformation Reflects Renewed Hope Agenda, Says Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has declared that the ongoing transformation of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) is a reflection of his administration’s Renewed Hope Agenda. He assured Nigerians that similar infrastructure development would be replicated across cities, satellite towns and rural

item.title: Deputy Speaker backs state police, faults Abuja-centred policing
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Benjamin Kalu, has backed the establishment of state police, arguing that Nigeria's centralised policing structure has failed to respond effectively to the country's security challenges. The post Deputy Speaker backs state police, faults Abuja-centred

item.title: Collateral consequences of Tinubu’s healthcare reforms, By Okey Akpa

item.description: The experience of the past three years suggests, however, that reforms on this scale can achieve more than better health outcomes. By creating a more coherent health system, attracting investment, expanding domestic manufacturing, strengthening human capital and building scientific and institutional

item.title: Air raid sirens in Bahrain after uneasy night of US-Iran strikes
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: After a tense night of overnight US-led strikes on Iran, air raid alerts sounded across Gulf states.

item.description: Medical drama The Pitt emerged as the biggest contender for the 78th Emmy Awards after securing 25 nominations when this year’s shortlist was announced in Los Angeles on Wednesday. Comedy series Hacks followed closely with 24 nominations, while Widow’s Bay received 19 nods and Pluribus e

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Team Nigeria has stepped up preparations for the 2026 Commonwealth Games, with the men’s 3×3 basketball team holding a final overseas training camp in Amsterdam, Netherlands, ahead of the competition in Glasgow, Scotland. item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Turkish tennis player Zeynep Sonmez defies a Wimbledon ban to stand in solidarity with Palestine.

item.title: Serbia Holds Rates as Election Welfare Plans Signal Price Risks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Serbia kept borrowing costs unchanged for a record 22nd month as a government plan for cash handouts before this year’s general election may add to inflation pressures.

item.description: In a bid to assist the Federal Government and Security Agencies in tackling issues of sea piracy and illegal oil bunkering along the waterways, the Tugboat, Fuel barges and seagoing vessel association of Nigeria has called for the immediate censorship, verification, and stricter control of all marin

item.title: ‘The money would have been life-changing’: My father told my grandmother to slash my inheritance. Is that fair?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I could have bought a home for what I hope will be a growing family.”

item.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

item.title: Troops intercept vehicle conveying suspected stolen livestock in Kebbi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the 8 Division, Nigerian Army, in collaboration with members of the Forest Guards, have intercepted a vehicle conveying suspected stolen livestock in Arewa Local Government Area of Kebbi State. The development was contained in a post shared by security analyst and counter-insurgency expert

item.title: Senate approves Customs’ 2026 budget, backs N11.074trn revenue target
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The proposed N1.295 trillion expenditure consists of N421 billion for personnel costs, N307 billion for overheads and N565 billion for capital projects. The post Senate approves Customs’ 2026 budget, backs N11.074trn revenue target appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

The deal, which progressed rapidly after United expressed interest ov

item.title: Flight school instructor jumps from plane, dies
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A 42-year-old flight school instructor has died after reportedly jumping from a training aircraft mid-flight in Argentina’s Córdoba province. In a statement, the country’s public prosecutor’s office said the incident occurred over the small town of Toledo in central Argentina on July 4 when Leandro

item.title: 600 dead in DR Congo Ebola outbreak
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has now claimed 600 lives, figures published by the World Health Organization showed Thursday -- only three days after the figure topped 500. The post 600 dead in DR Congo Ebola outbreak appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Iran to bury slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as week-long funeral ceremonies draw to a close
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Iran's slain ruler Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Thursday will be buried at the country's holiest shrine in Mashhad after a week of mass funeral processions, rallies and mourning ceremonies that has coincided ​with a flare-up in conflict with the US. Khamenei's son and successor, Supreme Leader Mojtaba

item.title: Police Kill Two Suspected Kidnappers After Rescue of Abducted Ogun Prince
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Ogun State Police Command says it has neutralised two suspected kidnappers and recovered an AK-47 rifle, mobile phones and other exhibits following an intelligence-led operation launched after the abduction of the Prince of Okun Owa, Prince Idris. The post Police Kill Two Suspected Kidnappers Af

item.description: "That is nonsensical. President Tinubu does not have the capacity to be in charge. I was shocked when..." The post Tinubu lacks capacity to lead Nigeria — Peter Obi appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.description: The United States government has published the names and photographs of 124 Nigerians it said will be deported over criminal convictions. The post FULL LIST: US govt publishes names, photos of 124 Nigerians to be deported for crimes appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Henderson Could Still Play At World Cup After Surgery
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: England midfielder Jordan Henderson has not been ruled out of further participation in the World Cup despite undergoing surgery on a broken arm, with team officials exploring the possibility of him playing in a cast. The 36-year-old Brentford midfielder sustained the freak injury while attempting to

item.title: Iran summons UK ambassador over stabbing allegation
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Two Romanians, convicted of a 2024 knife attack on an Iranian journalist outside his London home "on behalf of" Iran, were sentenced last week to 12 and eight years in prison respectively. The post Iran summons UK ambassador over stabbing allegation appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Major drugmaker loses $27 billion in market value after failed trial
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch

item.description: Newcastle captain, Bruno Guimaraes, has informed the Premier League club of his desire to join Arsenal. item.title: Hostage rescued safely as Ogun Police neutralise two abductors
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Babaseyi said the prince was abducted from his residence in Okun-Owa at about 7:30 p.m. on June 30, prompting the immediate deployment of the command’s Anti-Kidnapping Unit and other tactical teams.

item.title: How Fraudsters Attempted to Hijack GTA VI Preorders
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Following the official launch of Grand Theft Auto VI game pre-orders late June, which attracted significant attention, threat actors quickly began exploiting the event as a lure. Scammers leveraged the heightened demand to deploy deceptive schemes, from fake pre-order pages to fraudulent video tutor

item.title: TCN Completes Live-line Repair On Zaria–Funtua 132kV Without Disrupting Power Supply
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Transmission Company of Nigeria (TCN) said its Kaduna live-line crew carried out maintenance on the Zaria–Funtua 132kV transmission line without disrupting bulk power supply. TCN’s general manager of public affairs, Ndidi Mbah, said the exercise ran from Saturday, July 4 to Tuesday, July 7, 2026

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Chelsea have announced the signing of Sporting CP winger Geovany Quenda for £40 million, with the 19-year-old Portugal under-21 international signing an eight-year contract. Quenda becomes the second arrival under new manager Xabi Alonso, following £42m full-back Marco Palestra from Atalanta. The de

item.description: Prince Idris was kidnapped on June 30, 2026, at about 7:30 p.m. when armed men reportedly invaded his residence at Okun Owa and whisked him away. The post Police kill two suspected kidnappers, rescue Ogun prince appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Barclays Readies $875 Million Debt Sale for Buyout of Senior Plc
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Barclays Plc is preparing to sell about $875 million of debt financing used to fund a takeover of UK aerospace supplier Senior Plc.

item.title: Senate advances bill to strengthen fight against counterfeit drugs, proposes 15-year jail term for offenders
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Senate noted that counterfeit medicines have become silent weapons of mass destruction because they destroy lives, weaken healthcare delivery and undermine public confidence in genuine pharmaceutical products. The post Senate advances bill to strengthen fight against counterfeit drugs, proposes

item.title: Final Year UNIPORT Female Student Declared Missing
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A final-year student of the University of Port Harcourt (UNIPORT), popularly known as Princess Venita, has been reported missing by worried family and friends who want to know her whereabouts. Venita, a Facebook personality and model based in Port Harcourt, became an issue after family and friends n

item.title: Insurgency: Yobe Govs Says Collaboration With Security Agencies Yeilding Results
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Yobe State governor Mai Mala Buni has revealed the secret of the state’s successes in the war against insurgency. Buni who spoke at 16th African Business Leadership Awards (ABLA) in Westminster, London, said his administration’s collaboration with security agencies yielded positive resul

item.title: Lateef Adedimeji, Linda Ejiofor-Suleiman lead new Nollywood movie targeting Guinness World Record
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Black Market, starring Lateef Adedimeji and Linda Ejiofor-Suleiman, aims to break the Guinness World Record for a film screening. The post Lateef Adedimeji, Linda Ejiofor-Suleiman lead new Nollywood movie targeting Guinness World Record appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

item.title: Heartbroken Mother Cries Out After Ex‑Husband Allegedly Took Their Twin Daughters Away Since 2024
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: What kind of wickedness is this?A heartbroken mother has cried out that her ex‑husband allegedly took their twin daughters away in 2024 and has completely denied her access to them ever since.She says she has tried every possible means to be part of her children’s lives, but all efforts have been bl

item.title: States advocate transparent fibre duct pricing to support 90,000km rollout
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Governments of Kogi and Cross River States have advocated a transparent, cost-based pricing framework for shared fibre infrastructure, saying read more States advocate transparent fibre duct pricing to support 90,000km rollout

item.title: “Banditry, kidnappings are driving filmmakers away from epic movies” — Ibrahim Chatta
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The actor said the decline in epic productions has forced him to stop expanding his horse stable after years of supplying animals to Nollywood films. The post “Banditry, kidnappings are driving filmmakers away from epic movies” — Ibrahim Chatta appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Olukoyede lauded for EFCC’s strategy shift to proactive corruption prevention
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Public affairs analyst Chima Okoro has commended Ola Olukoyede, Chairman of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), for his read more Olukoyede lauded for EFCC’s strategy shift to proactive corruption prevention

item.title: Defense Valuations Yet to Peak as Orders Surge, BNP Banker Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: European defense sector valuations still have plenty of upside, with government pledges to boost military spending now translating into orders, according to a top banker at BNP Paribas SA.

item.title: 600 Dead In DR Congo Ebola Outbreak
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: A total of 285 patients in the DRC have recovered, while 304 suspected cases of the viral haemorrhagic fever are under investigation. The post 600 Dead In DR Congo Ebola Outbreak appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Tinubu will win by a landslide in 2027 — Umahi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: David Umahi says APC’s Ekiti election success reflects President Tinubu’s performance and predicts a landslide victory in 2027. The post Tinubu will win by a landslide in 2027 — Umahi appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.
